column1: # Container ARCHETYPES with typed content + material compatibility.
# A container is dedicated to certain contents. Putting the WRONG class of
# material into a container can DAMAGE or BREAK it (e.g. pouring cement into a
# dairy/liquid tank). The `accepted_content_classes` column is the whitelist of
# content classes this container can safely hold; anything else is incompatible
# and damages the vessel on each store attempt until it breaks.
# This is the infinite-of-X data file for container archetypes. To add a new

column2: append a row here - NEVER hardcode container lists in Rust.

column1: #   id                        - Unique container-type id (snake_case). Matches an item id
#                               in items.csv where category='container' when this archetype
#   base_material             - Primary material the container is made of (material id).
#   capacity_liters           - Maximum volume the container can hold
#   pressure_rating_atm       - Max internal pressure in atmospheres. 1.0 = ambient only
#                               (no pressurization). Higher = can hold compressed gas /
#                               cryogenic liquids that boil off and build pressure.
#   max_temp_c                - Highest content temperature the container tolerates (deg C).
#   min_temp_c                - Lowest content temperature the container tolerates (deg C).
#                               Cryogenic tanks go very low (e.g. -200) and are insulated.
#   accepted_content_classes  - Pipe-separated whitelist of content classes this container
#                               can safely hold (e.g. "liquid|water"). Matches ids in
#                               content_classes.ron. Anything NOT in this list is

column9: Slatted wooden crate for dry bulk solids and packaged goods
Flexible woven sack for grain seed and loose dry goods
Curved-stave barrel for water wine and non-corrosive liquids
Sealed steel drum for fuel oil and flammable liquids
Food-safe polyethylene cistern for potable water
Sealable glass jar for preserved food liquids and dry goods
High-pressure steel cylinder for compressed gases
Vacuum-insulated pressure tank for cryogenic liquids like liquid nitrogen or liquid CO2
Sealed chemical-resistant box for hazardous solids and toxic or corrosive materials
Food-grade stainless tank for milk and potable food liquids
Vertical bulk storage bin for grain and dry staples
